raw_text:
ONCOLOGY CONSULTATION NOTE - Date: 2026-02-10

Patient: 62-year-old Caucasian male referred for evaluation of progressive non-small cell lung cancer.

HPI: Patient was diagnosed with Stage IIIB adenocarcinoma of the right lung 14 months ago after presenting with persistent cough and hemoptysis. Initial biopsy confirmed adenocarcinoma, EGFR wild-type, ALK negative, ROS1 negative, PD-L1 TPS 60%. He completed 4 cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed with partial response, followed by 2 cycles of maintenance pemetrexed before progression was noted on restaging CT showing enlarging primary mass and new mediastinal lymphadenopathy. He is a former smoker with a 35 pack-year history, quit 8 years ago. He reports mild dyspnea on exertion, decreased appetite with 8 lb weight loss over 2 months, and intermittent right-sided chest discomfort.

Vitals: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 82 bpm, Temp 98.6F, RR 18, SpO2 94% RA. Weight 168 lbs, Height 5'11", BMI 23.4.

ECOG Performance Status: 1

Physical Exam: Thin male appearing stated age. Decreased breath sounds right middle and lower lobes. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpable. No hepatomegaly. No digital clubbing.

Labs: CBC within normal limits. WBC 6.8, Hgb 12.4, Plt 198. CMP unremarkable. LDH 245 U/L (mildly elevated). CEA 12.4 ng/mL.

Imaging: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (2026-02-05): 4.2 cm right hilar mass (previously 3.1 cm), subcarinal and right paratracheal lymphadenopathy. No distant metastases identified. Brain MRI negative.

Assessment and Plan:
1. Stage IIIB NSCLC (adenocarcinoma), progressive after first-line carboplatin/pemetrexed. Given PD-L1 TPS 60%, recommend pembrolizumab 200mg IV q3 weeks as second-line therapy.
2. Obtain molecular profiling to rule out actionable mutations (KRAS G12C, MET exon 14).
3. Supportive care: nutritional counseling, pulmonary rehabilitation.
4. Restaging CT in 9 weeks. Follow-up in 3 weeks after first cycle.
diagnoses: Stage IIIB non-small cell lung cancer (adenocarcinoma)
